label: PlanetScale
description: Connect to a PlanetScale MySQL-compatible serverless database using branch credentials from the PlanetScale Console.

form.title: Connect PlanetScale
form.subtitle: Paste your branch connection credentials from the PlanetScale Console to connect Anton to your database.

methods.label: Branch credentials (username & password)
methods.description: Paste the host, port, database name, username, and password generated in PlanetScale Console → Branch → Connect → New password. SSL is enforced automatically.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with PlanetScale branch credentials

PlanetScale generates per-branch credentials you can paste directly into Anton. SSL is required and enabled by default.

### Steps

1. Open [app.planetscale.com](https://app.planetscale.com) and sign in.
2. Select your **organization** and then your **database**.
3. In the left sidebar, click **Branches** and choose the branch you want Anton to connect to (e.g. `main`).
4. Click the **Connect** button in the top-right area of the branch page.
5. Click **New password** to generate a fresh set of credentials. Give it a name like `anton`.
6. In the **Connect with** dropdown, select **General** (or any MySQL-compatible option) to reveal the raw connection details.
7. Copy each of the following values into the fields below:
   - **Host** — e.g. `aws.connect.psdb.cloud`
   - **Port** — typically `3306`
   - **Database** — the name of your PlanetScale database
   - **Username** — the generated username
   - **Password** — the generated password (shown once — copy it now)

### Notes

- The password is shown **only once**. If you navigate away before copying, generate a new password.
- PlanetScale requires TLS/SSL — Anton always connects with SSL enabled for this connector.
- You can revoke credentials at any time from the **Passwords** tab on the branch page without affecting other credentials.
- Production branches may require a **safe migrations** workflow; ensure the branch allows the queries Anton will run.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: The hostname from PlanetScale Console → Branch → Connect. Usually ends in .psdb.cloud.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: The name of your PlanetScale database (matches the database name in the Console).

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: The generated username from PlanetScale Console → Branch → Connect → New password.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: The generated password — shown only once in the Console. Generate a new one if lost.

fields.name: ssl_enabled
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: PlanetScale requires SSL. Leave this enabled unless you have a specific reason to disable it.

methods.label: Connection URL
methods.description: Paste the full MySQL connection URL from PlanetScale Console directly. Anton will parse the host, port, database, credentials, and SSL settings from the URL string.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a PlanetScale connection URL

If you prefer a single connection string over individual fields, PlanetScale provides a ready-made URL.

### Steps

1. Open [app.planetscale.com](https://app.planetscale.com) and sign in.
2. Navigate to your **database → Branches → Connect → New password**.
3. In the **Connect with** dropdown, select **MySQL CLI** or **General** to reveal the connection URL.
4. Copy the full URL — it looks like:
   `mysql://username:password@aws.connect.psdb.cloud/my-database?ssl-mode=REQUIRED`
5. Paste it into the **Connection URL** field below.

### Notes

- The URL contains your password in plain text — treat it as a secret.
- The password is shown only once. If you missed it, generate a new credential set.
- SSL mode is embedded in the URL (`?ssl-mode=REQUIRED`). Do not remove that parameter.
- You can revoke the credential at any time from the **Passwords** tab on the branch page.

fields.name: connection_url
fields.label: Connection URL
fields.description: Full MySQL connection URL from PlanetScale Console → Branch → Connect. Keep the ?ssl-mode=REQUIRED parameter.
